title: Orchestration selection checklist — TELOS-local advisory synthesis
what: A five-step selection aid an author works through before committing to an orchestration shape. This is a TELOS-LOCAL SYNTHESIS derived from the cited guidance — it is NOT presented as a verbatim Anthropic checklist.
why: Operationalizes simplicity-first: the checklist steers toward the least-complex adequate pattern and forces the ground-truth-checkpoint question before any autonomous loop.
non_claim: Local synthesis, not a verbatim Anthropic checklist. It recommends; it does not enforce, does not choose the pattern for you, and creates no governing behavior. Any mandatory authorization or checkpoint remains mandatory only by its own TELOS/Argo authority-anchored record.
change_rule: A reviewed edit; the checker requires this record be explicitly marked local synthesis (synthesis: true) and carry evidence references.

steps.step: Define success and available evaluation evidence.
steps.detail: State what 'good' means and what signal (test, oracle, human review) can measure it. No evaluation signal is a warning against any pattern with a feedback loop or an autonomous agent — you would not be able to check its work.

steps.step: Test the simplest adequate form first.
steps.detail: Try a single augmented-LLM call (with retrieval/tools) before any multi-step orchestration. If it suffices, stop — do not orchestrate.

Sequential (prompt chaining), routable (routing), parallelizable (parallelization), dynamically decomposable (orchestrator-workers), or feedback-driven (evaluator-optimizer). Only genuinely open-ended, unpredictable-step tasks point at an autonomous agent.

steps.step: Select the least-complex pattern that addresses the demonstrated failure mode.
steps.detail: Add complexity only when evidence shows the simpler tested form is inadequate — not preemptively. More agentic is not more capable if the task did not need it.

steps.step: Define stopping conditions, checkpoint behavior, human escalation, and authorization boundaries proportional to risk.
steps.detail: Any autonomous or feedback loop needs an explicit stopping condition and a ground-truth checkpoint. In TELOS, irreversible/outward-facing steps route to The Eye and through the gate — but that requirement lives in the TELOS/Argo records, not here.
